entries.problem: Load CSV or Parquet with correct types from the start
entries.trigger: Reading raw data files where dtype inference causes downstream errors
entries.snippet:
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv', dtype={'id': str, 'score': float}, usecols=['id', 'score', 'label'])
df = pd.read_parquet('data.parquet')   # always typed correctly — prefer parquet
entries.minimal_notes: Always specify dtype on read_csv for key columns — inference from first 100 rows misses mixed types later in the file.
entries.common_bug: df.to_csv writes the index by default — always pass index=False.

entries.problem: Audit data quality after loading (nulls, types, shape)
entries.trigger: First thing after read_csv — before any filtering or modeling
entries.snippet:
df.info()                                         # shape, dtypes, null counts
df.isnull().sum().sort_values(ascending=False)    # ranked null distribution
df.describe(include='all')                        # stats for all column types
entries.minimal_notes: describe() excludes non-numeric columns unless include='all' is passed.
entries.common_bug: Skipping this step is the most common cause of silent type bugs — object-dtype columns that should be numeric pass all downstream operations but produce NaN silently.

entries.problem: Select rows by condition and columns by name in one operation
entries.trigger: Filtering training data by label, extracting feature columns for model input
entries.snippet:
subset = df.loc[df['age'] > 30, ['name', 'salary']]         # label-based
active  = df.query('status == "active" and score >= 0.8')    # readable multi-condition
entries.minimal_notes: Never chain brackets for assignment: df[mask]['col'] = x triggers SettingWithCopyWarning and may not write. Use df.loc[mask, 'col'] = x.
entries.common_bug: df.loc with integer labels on a reindexed DataFrame selects by label not position — use df.iloc for positional access.

entries.problem: Clean nulls, duplicates, and types before feature engineering
entries.trigger: Any raw dataset entering a pipeline — always required before modeling
entries.snippet:
df = df.dropna(subset=['label', 'text'])
df = df.fillna({'score': df['score'].median(), 'category': 'unknown'})
df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['user_id'], keep='first')
df = df.astype({'id': str, 'score': float})
entries.minimal_notes: Order: dropna → fillna → drop_duplicates → astype. Casting before handling nulls fails with ValueError on NaN columns.
entries.common_bug: fillna(0) on object-dtype column inserts the string '0', not the integer 0 — cast the column to numeric first.

entries.problem: Group by key column and aggregate multiple columns
entries.trigger: Per-class statistics, entity-level feature aggregation, reporting
entries.snippet:
result = df.groupby('region').agg({'sales': 'sum', 'profit': 'mean'}).reset_index()

# Time-series resampling — requires DatetimeIndex
monthly = df.set_index('date').resample('M')['value'].mean()
entries.minimal_notes: For categoricals, always pass observed=True to groupby — otherwise phantom groups with NaN aggregates appear for all categories.
entries.common_bug: resample requires a DatetimeIndex — calling it on a string or integer index raises TypeError without telling you to set the index first.
